anti_claims: This skill JSON seed does not flip source authority away from the current skill markdown projection.
Resolved operated-standard and acted-on-kind edges are structural route mappings, not proof the skill is complete or used correctly by agents.
Absent mechanism or concept neighbours are residual pressure, not evidence that no such neighbours exist.
Generated graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

omitted: mechanism or concept neighbours not named by typed source mapping
omission_receipt.reason: Skill instance preserves public-safe markdown source identity, digest, and typed route mapping only. JSON parity does not prove full skill-body completeness or runtime correctness.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Bind skill.uses mechanism and skill.applies concept edges only when source evidence names ids.

justification.summary: This markdown binds mechanism authoring to std_microcosm_mechanism, the mechanism doctrine kind, and the executable doctrine grammar mechanism because mechanism admission must be computed from source rows rather than asserted from prose.

title: Microcosm Mechanism Author
triggers: A source-backed operational transformation needs a governed mechanism instance.
The agent can name the mechanism source row, code locus or typed residual, host organ or typed residual, and claim ceiling boundary.
workflow: Read std_microcosm_mechanism, the relation registry, mechanism_sources, code loci, host organ evidence, and projection health before editing.
Add only source-backed mechanism rows and let the doctrine-lattice builder generate mechanism JSON, markdown, graph, and health surfaces.
Run mechanism corpus checks, projection checks, and focused runtime tests before scoped commit.
